rationale: My round-1 §1-vs-§3 note is ADDRESSED. Verified against the real ADR-0037: §1 ('Registration: panel.json is the panel's identity') is the schema block holding the abandon_reason field and the 099/102/106 registration amendments; §3's 2026-07-07 spec-109 note self-describes as 'a genuine extension of this section's [threshold] rule'. Bead 2 step 3 now homes the decision-inert `gate` note under §1 with exactly that precedent, §§3/6/8 untouched. No ADR/contract regression in the fix delta: config.go:149 is the `count:` render in the panel-reviewers loop; the CountValue() deref is orthogonal to threshold resolution, which stays single-homed in internal/panel.Panel.ApproveThreshold (the sibling approve_threshold render uses raw PanelApproveThresholdExpr()). Leaf invariant intact — live `go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep -c internal/config` = 0; the delta touches cmd/mindspec, internal/complete, internal/config only, and Beads 2/3 re-assert the grep. Bead 3 nil-guard is advisory-path only; Allow/Block and §6 fail-open unchanged. report.go's r.Count correctly fenced as a distinct struct.

findings.detail: Amendment note moved to §1 (schema block, abandon_reason precedent), not §3; the 109 note stayed in §3 because it extended the threshold rule, which the decision-inert `gate` field does not. Placement is now correct.

config.go:149 CountValue() deref renders reviewer count only; threshold render below uses raw PanelApproveThresholdExpr(), resolution single-homed in internal/panel.Panel.ApproveThreshold per ADR-0037 §3.
Live go list -deps count = 0; fix delta adds no internal/panel dependency (touches cmd/mindspec + internal/complete + internal/config); Beads 2 and 3 re-assert the grep.
Bead 3 nil-guard is on the advisory (reviewerCountAdvisory) path only; the panelGate Allow/Block already computed upstream is untouched, §6 fail-open-without-a-panel preserved.

With 099/102/106 already in §1 and 109 in §3, the 112 note is the fifth overall; 'second' is spec-inherited Validation-Proof language describing it relative to 109's field extension. The grep-based ACs do not depend on the ordinal. Not a regression, not a blocker.
